mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth signals the pace of economic expansion, and sustained GDP growth above 2% drives corporate earnings higher, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The rank IC of GDPC1 growth on forward 20-day returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 4.5%, monetary policy becomes restrictive, increasing borrowing costs and discount rates, which depresses equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of instances triggered by FEDFUNDS > 4.5, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising consumer prices and declining industrial production signals stagflation, where cost-push inflation erodes margins and weak production limits output, causing negative equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL_change * INDPRO_change) in a predictive regression does not have a statistically significant negative coefficient (p>0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In low interest rate regimes (FEDFUNDS < 3%), rising retail sales reflect strong consumer demand which directly boosts corporate revenues and stock prices, producing positive returns over the short term.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS monthly changes and forward 5-day returns is not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS < 3% compared to when FEDFUNDS >= 3% (z-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market with rising incomes, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The rank IC of PAYEMS monthly changes on forward 20-day returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals deteriorating conditions in the housing sector, which often precedes broader economic slowdown and dampens equity market confidence over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of months when HOUST < 1200, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When high inflation (CPI y/y > 3%) coincides with strong payroll growth, it creates wage-price spiral fears, prompting tighter monetary policy and margin compression, leading to negative equity returns over the month.
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL_yoy * PAYEMS_mom) in a predictive regression is not significantly negative (p>0.10) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS rising over 3 months), rising unemployment becomes a more potent negative signal as it indicates that the economy is losing momentum under tighter financial conditions, leading to short-term equity declines.
failure_condition: The correlation between weekly UNRATE changes and forward 5-day returns is not more negative during tightening cycles than during non-tightening cycles (difference of correlations fails t-test p<0.05).
